compiled_state.title: Wire recipe library into implementation planning and sprint execution
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Epic A (dso-qo0x) delivers the recipe registry, execution engine, and initial catalog as standalone infrastructure. This epic wires that library into the DSO workflow: /dso:implementation-plan consults the registry during approach design, /dso:sprint executes recipe: tasks as shell commands (no sub-agent dispatch), and a post-implementation cleanup phase runs applicable recipes before code review.

## Success Criteria

1. /dso:implementation-plan consults the recipe registry during approach design and assigns recipe-backed tasks where a deterministic transform is a better fit than LLM generation — verified by a plan that uses a recipe task for a mechanical signature change while keeping business logic as an LLM task, without splitting work artificially to maximize recipe usage
2. Sprint executes recipe: tasks as shell commands with the recipe executor — verified by sprint log showing deterministic command execution for recipe tasks
3. A post-implementation cleanup phase runs applicable cleanup recipes after each sub-agent returns and before code review, resolving mechanical defects deterministically — verified by cleanup recipe fixing import ordering before review captures the diff
4. Pre-flight engine check runs at sprint start, validating all engines referenced in the plan before any tasks execute — verified by pre-flight detecting missing engine and surfacing it before first task
5. When an engine is not installed at sprint time, recipe tasks gracefully degrade to LLM sub-agent tasks with a ticket comment recording the fallback — verified by test confirming fallback + ticket comment

## Dependencies

- Depends on dso-qo0x (recipe registry and execution engine)
- Coordinate with dso-to7x (worktree isolation): recipe tasks in worktree context must use worktree cwd
- Coordinate with dso-t7af (usage-aware throttling): LLM fallback tasks count toward usage
